briefing:
## 🌍 Top 5 Global Fintech News

- **[Fintech founder charged with fraud; AI app found to be humans in the Philippines](https://techcrunch.com/2025/04/10/fintech-founder-charged-with-fraud-after-ai-shopping-app-found-to-be-powered-by-humans-in-the-philippines/)** — *Hacker News* — A fintech founder faces fraud charges after their supposedly AI-powered shopping app was revealed to be operated by human workers in the Philippines.

- **[Americans see their savings vanish in Synapse fintech crisis](https://www.cnbc.com/2024/11/22/synapse-bankruptcy-thousands-of-americans-see-their-savings-vanish.html)** — *Hacker News* — Thousands of Americans lost access to their savings following the collapse of Banking-as-a-Service middleware provider Synapse, highlighting critical risks in embedded finance infrastructure.

- **[Goodbye Visa and Mastercard: 130M Europeans switching to sovereign payment](https://www.lesnumeriques.com/banque-en-ligne/adieu-visa-et-mastercard-130-millions-d-europeens-basculent-vers-un-paiement-100-souverain-des-2026-n250918.html)** — *Hacker News* — Over 130 million Europeans are transitioning to a sovereign payment network, signalling a major shift away from Visa and Mastercard dominance in Europe.

- **[Apple acquires UK open banking startup Credit Kudos](https://ffnews.com/newsarticle/apple-acquires-uk-open-banking-startup-credit-kudos/)** — *Hacker News* — Apple's acquisition of open banking startup Credit Kudos points to Big Tech's growing ambitions in financial data and credit decisioning.

- **[Open Banking and Payments Competition](https://www.bitsaboutmoney.com/archive/open-banking-and-payments-competition/)** — *Hacker News* — A deep-dive analysis explores how open banking frameworks are reshaping competition in the payments landscape globally.

---

## 🍁 Top 5 Canadian Fintech News

- **[Hot bet summer? Soon Canadians will be able to trade on prediction markets](https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/prediction-markets-wealthsimple-9.7239575?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Wealthsimple and others are set to offer Canadians access to prediction markets, opening a new frontier in retail financial products.

- **['Take Risk': Canada Regulator Cuts Bank Capital Level to Boost Lending](https://financialpost.com/pmn/business-pmn/take-risk-canada-regulator-cuts-bank-capital-level-to-boost-lending)** — *Financial Post* — OSFI has lowered the domestic stability buffer for Canada's largest banks for the first time in three years, freeing up capacity for increased lending.

- **[Questrade launches custom indexing as massive IPOs reshape the market](https://betakit.com/questrade-launches-custom-indexing-as-massive-ipos-reshape-the-market/)** — *BetaKit* — Canadian investment platform Questrade is rolling out custom indexing tools, expanding its wealth management offerings amid a wave of major market activity.

- **[Canada calls screen scraping 'unsecure,' sets Open Banking target for 2023](https://finledger.com/2021/08/09/canada-sets-open-banking-target-for-2023/)** — *Hacker News* — Canada's government flagged screen scraping as a security risk and outlined its ambitions for a regulated open banking framework — context still highly relevant as implementation continues to evolve.

- **[How IDMTC and SR&ED can help Canadian tech companies stretch their R&D budgets](https://betakit.com/how-idmtc-and-sred-can-help-canadian-tech-companies-stretch-their-rd-budgets/)** — *BetaKit* — A practical guide for Canadian tech and fintech startups on leveraging federal tax incentives to maximize research and development spending.

---

## 🌊 Bonus: Relevant to Atlantic Fintech's Focus (1–3 articles)

- **[Regulator cuts big banks' capital buffer for the first time in three years](https://financialpost.com/fp-finance/banking/osfi-cuts-bank-domestic-stability-buffer)** — *Financial Post* — OSFI's decision to reduce capital requirements could encourage more lending activity across Canada, including in Atlantic Canada where access to credit remains a key challenge for small businesses and fintech-adjacent sectors.

- **[Cybersecurity coding bootcamp aims to help Canadian veterans transition to tech jobs](https://betakit.com/cybersecurity-coding-bootcamp-aims-to-help-canadian-veterans-transition-to-tech-jobs/)** — *BetaKit* — A new cybersecurity bootcamp targeting Canadian veterans aligns with Atlantic Canada's growing focus on workforce development in tech, a critical enabler for the regional fintech ecosystem.

- **[Fintech is making credit cards weirder](https://workweek.com/2022/08/05/fintech-is-making-credit-cards-weirder/)** — *Hacker News* — As fintechs continue to innovate around credit products, this piece is relevant to Atlantic Canadian credit unions and fintechs exploring differentiated lending and card offerings for underserved markets.
